title: rosa canina var. cladoleia
intro: Rosa canina var. cladoleia, also known as the dwarf wild rose, is a compact and shrub-like variety of the wild rose plant that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant compounds that can help to soothe and heal skin irritations, wounds, and other health issues. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina var. cladoleia have been traditionally used to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system, among other health benefits. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a variety of health conditions, including eczema, acne, and minor wounds.
